docs.title: naste
docs.text:
A minimal, self-hosted paste service for the command line. No database. No frameworks. Just files.

Host your own for privacy or provide it as a free service to others.

docs.title: At a Glance
docs.text: Language Go (stdlib only, zero dependencies) Storage Filesystem (no database) Binaries naste-server (server), naste (CLI client) Deployment Docker, Nix, NixOS module, Home Manager module Security Path traversal prevention, atomic writes, constant-time auth Size limit 10 MB per paste

docs.title: CLI Client
docs.text:
The naste binary sends text to a paste server and returns a URL. Install it on any machine to push pastes.

GoNix (run without install)NixOS moduleHome Manager 
go install github.com/semi710/naste/cmd/naste@latest
echo "hello world" | naste

echo "hello world" | nix run github:semi710/naste#naste --

programs.naste-client = {
  enable = true;
  endpoint = "https://paste.semi.sh";
};

programs.naste-client = {
  enable = true;
  endpoint = "https://paste.semi.sh";
};

docs.title: Server
docs.text:
The naste-server binary hosts the paste service on port 8080. It stores pastes as files on disk. No database, no dependencies.

DockerNixBinaryNixOS moduleHome Manager 
docker run -d \
  --name naste \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  -v /var/lib/naste/data:/data/paste \
  ghcr.io/semi710/naste-server:latest

# Default settings (port 8080, data in /data/paste)
nix run github:semi710/naste#naste-server

# With custom env vars
PORT=9090 DATA_DIR=/tmp/naste-data nix run github:semi710/naste#naste-server

go build -o naste-server .
PORT=8080 DATA_DIR=./data ./naste-server

services.naste-server = {
  enable = true;
  port = 8080;
  openFirewall = true;
};

services.naste-server = {
  enable = true;
  port = 8080;
};

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Pipe any text
echo "hello world" | naste
# -> https://paste.semi.sh/abc123

# With a custom slug
cat deploy.sh | naste --slug deploy

# Private paste (requires server auth config)
cat secrets.env | naste --private --slug secrets

# Force overwrite existing slug
naste updated.go --force --slug mycode

# Auto-detects language from file extension
naste main.go

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Zero Database - Filesystem-based storage. No PostgreSQL, no MongoDB, no Redis.
  • Private Pastes - HTTP Basic Auth for sensitive content. No accounts needed.
  • Custom Slugs - Readable URLs like /deploy-script instead of random IDs.
  • Syntax Highlighting - Auto-detects language from file extension. Dark theme in browser.
  • Single Binary - One Go binary. Deploy anywhere in seconds.
  • Docker Ready - Multi-arch images (amd64 + arm64) via GitHub Actions.
  • NixOS Module - Declarative system service with sops-nix secret support.
  • Home Manager - Run as a user service, no root required.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
+-------------+
                    |   naste     |  CLI client
                    |  (binary)   |  reads ~/.config/naste/config.toml
                    +-----+-------+
                          |
                    POST /api/paste
                    GET  /{slug}
                          |
                    +-----+-------+
                    | naste-server|  HTTP server
                    |  (binary)   |  port 8080
                    +-----+-------+
                          |
                    +-----+-------+
                    | filesystem  |  /data/paste/
                    |  storage    |  public/ private/ metadata/
                    +-------------+

The server stores pastes as flat files in three directories:

  • public/ - public paste content
  • private/ - private paste content (auth-gated)
  • metadata/ - JSON metadata per paste (slug, lang, size, timestamps)

All writes are atomic (temp file + rename). No partial states.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
The CLI reads configuration from (in order of precedence):

  1. PASTE_ENDPOINT env var
  2. PASTE_USER / PASTE_PASS / PASTE_USER_FILE / PASTE_PASS_FILE env vars
  3. ~/.config/naste/config.toml
  4. Built-in default: https://paste.semi.sh
# ~/.config/naste/config.toml
endpoint = "https://paste.semi.sh"
user = "admin"
password = "secret"

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• CLI Usage - All flags and options
  • Docker - Container deployment
  • Nix - Nix flake usage
  • NixOS - System service with sops-nix, all options
  • Home Manager - User service, all options
  • Reverse Proxy - Caddy, Nginx, Tailscale
  • API Reference - HTTP endpoints

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: All endpoints accept and return raw text or JSON. No authentication required for public pastes.

docs.title: Create Paste
docs.text: POST /api/paste

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Header Required Description X-Slug No Custom slug (random if not provided) X-Private No Set to true for private paste X-Lang No Language for syntax highlighting

docs.title: Body
docs.text: Raw text content of the paste.

docs.title: Response: 201 Created
docs.text:
{
  "url": "https://paste.semi.sh/abc123"
}

For private pastes:

{
  "url": "https://paste.semi.sh/private/abc123"
}

docs.title: Response: 409 Conflict
docs.text:
{
  "error": "slug_exists"
}

docs.title: Response: 403 Forbidden
docs.text:
{
  "error": "private_auth_not_configured"
}

Returned when X-Private: true is sent but the server has no PRIVATE_USER/PRIVATE_PASS configured.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Create paste
curl -X POST https://paste.semi.sh/api/paste -d "hello world"

# With custom slug
curl -X POST https://paste.semi.sh/api/paste \
  -H "X-Slug: mycode" \
  -d "content here"

# Private paste
curl -X POST https://paste.semi.sh/api/paste \
  -H "X-Private: true" \
  -H "X-Lang: go" \
  -d "package main"

# From file
curl -X POST https://paste.semi.sh/api/paste \
  -H "X-Slug: deploy" \
  -H "X-Lang: bash" \
  --data-binary @deploy.sh

docs.title: Overwrite Paste
docs.text:
PUT /api/paste/{slug}

Replaces the content of an existing paste. For private pastes, requires Basic Auth.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "status": "updated"
}

docs.title: Response: 401 Unauthorized
docs.text: WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="Private Paste"

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Overwrite public paste
curl -X PUT https://paste.semi.sh/api/paste/mycode -d "updated content"

# Overwrite private paste (requires auth)
curl -X PUT -u admin:secret https://paste.semi.sh/api/paste/secrets -d "new secrets"

docs.title: Get Public Paste
docs.text: GET /{slug}

docs.title: Browser (HTML)
docs.text: Returns an HTML page with syntax highlighting (dark theme). The User-Agent header is checked to determine if the request is from a browser.

docs.title: curl / tools (raw text)
docs.text: Returns raw text with Content-Type: text/plain.

docs.title: Force raw text
docs.text:
GET /{slug}?raw=1

Always returns raw text regardless of User-Agent.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Browser: Content-Type: text/html curl: Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

docs.title: Response: 404 Not Found
docs.text: Paste does not exist or is private.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Browser: HTML with syntax highlighting
curl -s https://paste.semi.sh/abc123 | head

# Force raw text
curl -s https://paste.semi.sh/abc123?raw=1

docs.title: Get Private Paste
docs.text:
GET /private/{slug}

Returns raw text content. Requires HTTP Basic Auth.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Content-Type: text/plain

docs.title: Response: 401 Unauthorized
docs.text: WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="Private Paste"

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: curl -u admin:secret https://paste.semi.sh/private/secrets

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text: GET /health

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "status": "ok"
}

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: curl -s https://paste.semi.sh/health

docs.title: Landing Page
docs.text:
GET /

Returns a minimal HTML page with usage instructions and a link to the source repository.

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
All errors return JSON with an error field:

Status Error Cause 400 invalid_slug Slug contains invalid characters or is reserved 403 private_auth_not_configured Private paste requested but server has no auth 404 (empty) Paste not found 409 slug_exists Slug already in use 413 (empty) Paste exceeds 10 MB limit 500 (empty) Server error

docs.title: Slug Validation
docs.text:
Slugs must:

  • Be 1-64 characters long
  • Contain only [a-zA-Z0-9_-]
  • Not be a reserved word: private, api, health
  • Not contain path traversal sequences (.., /, \)

docs.title: CLI Usage
docs.text: The naste CLI client sends text to a paste server and returns a URL.

docs.title: Installation
Flags
docs.text: Flag Shorthand Description --slug <name> -s Custom slug for the paste --private -p Create a private paste (requires auth) --force -f Force overwrite if slug exists --lang <lang> -l Language for syntax highlighting (auto-detected from file extension if not set) --version -v Print version and exit --help -h Show help

docs.title: Pipe from stdin
docs.text:
echo "hello world" | naste
cat file.go | naste
kubectl get pods | naste -s k8s-pods

docs.title: File argument
docs.text:
Pass a file path as a positional argument. Language is auto-detected from the extension.

naste main.go
naste deploy.sh
naste config.yaml

docs.title: Config file
docs.text:
Create ~/.config/naste/config.toml:

endpoint = "https://paste.semi.sh"
user = "admin"
password = "secret"

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
Environment variables override the config file:

Variable Description PASTE_ENDPOINT Server URL (default: https://paste.semi.sh) PASTE_USER Username for private pastes PASTE_PASS Password for private pastes PASTE_USER_FILE File containing username (overrides PASTE_USER) PASTE_PASS_FILE File containing password (overrides PASTE_PASS)

File-based credentials take precedence over inline env vars. File contents are trimmed of whitespace. Use with sops-nix for secret management.

docs.title: Language Detection
docs.text:
Auto-detected from file extension when using file arguments. Override with --lang.

Extension Language .go go .py .pyw python .js .jsx .mjs javascript .ts .tsx typescript .rs rust .c .h c .cpp .cxx .cc .hpp cpp .sh .bash bash .nix nix .yaml .yml yaml ... 30+ languages total

docs.title: Overwrite Behavior
docs.text:
If a slug already exists and --force is not passed, the CLI prompts interactively:

$ naste file.go -s mycode
Slug 'mycode' exists. Override? [y/N]

The prompt reads from /dev/tty (not stdin), so it works even when piping content.

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 Error (network, file read, server rejection)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Quick paste
echo "hello" | naste

# Named paste with language
cat script.sh | naste -s deploy -l bash

# Private paste
cat secrets.env | naste -p -s secrets

# Overwrite existing
naste updated.go -f -s mycode

# Pipe kubectl output
kubectl get pods -o yaml | naste -s k8s-debug

docs.title: Docker Deployment
docs.text: The Docker image is built via Nix for reproducibility and minimal size. Multi-arch manifests (amd64 + arm64) are published to GitHub Container Registry.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
docker run -d \
  --name naste \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  -v /var/lib/naste/data:/data/paste \
  ghcr.io/semi710/naste-server:latest

docs.title: With Private Paste Auth
docs.text:
docker run -d \
  --name naste \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  -e PRIVATE_USER=admin \
  -e PRIVATE_PASS=your-secret-password \
  -v /var/lib/naste/data:/data/paste \
  --read-only \
  --cap-drop ALL \
  --security-opt no-new-privileges:true \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  ghcr.io/semi710/naste-server:latest

docs.title: With Secret Files
docs.text:
For Docker Swarm or Kubernetes, mount secret files instead of passing credentials as env vars:

docker run -d \
  --name naste \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  -e PRIVATE_USER_FILE=/run/secrets/naste-user \
  -e PRIVATE_PASS_FILE=/run/secrets/naste-pass \
  -v /var/lib/naste/data:/data/paste \
  -v ./secrets/user:/run/secrets/naste-user:ro \
  -v ./secrets/pass:/run/secrets/naste-pass:ro \
  ghcr.io/semi710/naste-server:latest

File vars take precedence over inline env vars. File contents are trimmed of whitespace.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Default Description PORT 8080 HTTP listen port DATA_DIR /data/paste Storage directory PRIVATE_USER (empty) Username for private pastes PRIVATE_PASS (empty) Password for private pastes PRIVATE_USER_FILE (empty) File containing username (overrides PRIVATE_USER) PRIVATE_PASS_FILE (empty) File containing password (overrides PRIVATE_PASS) MAX_PASTE_SIZE 10485760 Maximum paste size in bytes (default: 10 MB)

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
services:
  naste:
    image: ghcr.io/semi710/naste-server:latest
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - naste-data:/data/paste
    environment:
      PRIVATE_USER: admin
      PRIVATE_PASS: ${NASTE_PASS}
    read_only: true
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  naste-data:

docs.title: Building the Image Locally
docs.text:
# Build via Nix
nix build .#dockerImage

# Load into Docker
docker load < result

# The image is tagged as ghcr.io/semi710/naste-server:latest
docker run -p 8080:8080 ghcr.io/semi710/naste-server:latest

docs.title: Image Details
docs.text: Base Nix built layered image User UID 1000 (non-root) Working dir /data/paste Exposed port 8080/tcp Volume /data/paste Architectures linux/amd64, linux/arm64

docs.title: Home Manager
docs.text: The Home Manager module runs naste-server as a user systemd service. No root required. Also installs the naste CLI client.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Add the flake input and overlay:

{
  inputs.naste.url = "github:semi710/naste";

  outputs = { self, home-manager, naste, ... }: {
    homeConfigurations.myuser = home-manager.lib.homeManagerConfiguration {
      modules = [
        naste.homeModules.default
        {
          nixpkgs.overlays = [ naste.overlays.default ];
          # ... service config here
        }
      ];
    };
  };
}

The overlay provides pkgs.naste-server and pkgs.naste.

docs.title: Quick Setup
docs.text:
{
  services.naste-server = {
    enable = true;
    port = 8080;
    privateUser = "admin";
    privatePass = "secret";
  };

  programs.naste-client = {
    enable = true;
    endpoint = "https://paste.semi.sh";
  };
}

docs.title: With NixOS + Home Manager
docs.text:
If you use Home Manager as a NixOS module, add the HM module to sharedModules:

home-manager.sharedModules = [
  naste.homeModules.default
];

Then enable in your host config:

hm.services.naste-server = {
  enable = true;
  port = 8080;
  privateUser = "admin";
  privatePass = "secret";
};

docs.title: With sops-nix
docs.text:
hm.services.naste-server = {
  enable = true;
  port = 8080;
  privateUserFile = config.hm.sops.secrets."naste/user".path;
  privatePassFile = config.hm.sops.secrets."naste/pass".path;
};
hm.sops.secrets."naste/user" = {
  sopsFile = ./secrets/server.yaml;
};
hm.sops.secrets."naste/pass" = {
  sopsFile = ./secrets/server.yaml;
};

docs.title: services.naste-server.enable
docs.text:
Whether to enable the naste paste service as a user systemd service.

Type: boolean Default: false Example: true

docs.title: services.naste-server.port
docs.text:
HTTP listen port.

Type: port Default: 8080 Example: 9090

docs.title: services.naste-server.dataDir
docs.text:
Storage directory for paste content.

Type: path Default: ~/.local/share/naste-server/data Example: /mnt/storage/naste

Data persistence

Pastes are stored as flat files and never automatically purged. No TTL or expiry.

docs.title: services.naste-server.maxPasteSize
docs.text:
Maximum paste size in bytes.

Type: positive integer Default: 10485760 (10 MB) Example: 52428800 (50 MB)

docs.title: services.naste-server.privateUser
docs.text:
Username for private paste authentication.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "admin"

docs.title: services.naste-server.privatePass
docs.text:
Password for private paste authentication.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "secret"

docs.title: services.naste-server.privateUserFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the username. Overrides privateUser.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/user"

docs.title: services.naste-server.privatePassFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the password. Overrides privatePass.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/pass"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.enable
docs.text:
Whether to install the naste CLI client to home packages and set session variables.

Type: boolean Default: false Example: true

docs.title: programs.naste-client.endpoint
docs.text:
Paste server endpoint URL. Required when enabled. The build fails with an assertion error if not set.

Type: string Default: none (assertion error if unset) Example: "https://paste.semi.sh"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privateUser
docs.text:
Username for private pastes. Sets the PASTE_USER session variable.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "admin"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privateUserFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the username. Overrides privateUser. Sets the PASTE_USER_FILE session variable.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/user"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privatePass
docs.text:
Password for private pastes. Sets the PASTE_PASS session variable.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "secret"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privatePassFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the password. Overrides privatePass. Sets the PASTE_PASS_FILE session variable.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/pass"

docs.title: Verifying
docs.text:
systemctl --user status naste-server
curl -s http://localhost:8080/health
journalctl --user -u naste-server -f

docs.title: Differences from NixOS Module
docs.text: Feature NixOS Module Home Manager Module Service type System service User service Requires root Yes No Firewall openFirewall option Not available User/group Creates naste system user Runs as your user Data dir /var/lib/naste-server/data ~/.local/share/naste-server/data CLI client programs.naste-client programs.naste-client (same) Env vars environment.variables home.sessionVariables

docs.title: Nix Deployment
docs.text: The naste flake provides packages, apps, an overlay, and modules for Nix-based deployment.

docs.title: Flake Outputs
docs.text: Output Type Description packages.default derivation naste-server binary packages.naste-server derivation Same as default (alias) packages.naste derivation naste CLI binary packages.dockerImage derivation Docker image tarball apps.naste app Run naste CLI directly apps.deploy app SSH/local deploy script overlays.default overlay Adds naste-server and naste to pkgs nixosModules.default module NixOS system service + CLI client homeModules.default module Home Manager user service

docs.title: Server
docs.text:
# Default settings (port 8080, data in /data/paste)
nix run github:semi710/naste#naste-server

# With custom env vars
PORT=9090 DATA_DIR=/tmp/naste-data nix run github:semi710/naste#naste-server

docs.title: CLI
docs.text: echo "hello" | nix run github:semi710/naste#naste --

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
# Build server binary
nix build github:semi710/naste

# Build CLI binary
nix build github:semi710/naste#naste

# Build Docker image
nix build github:semi710/naste#dockerImage

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
# Install CLI to profile
nix profile install github:semi710/naste#naste

# Install server to profile
nix profile install github:semi710/naste

docs.title: Deploy Script
docs.text:
The flake includes a deploy app that wraps Docker deployment with config persistence:

# Deploy locally (prompts for config on first run)
nix run github:semi710/naste#deploy

# Deploy to remote server via SSH
nix run github:semi710/naste#deploy -- user@server

# Deploy from GitHub without cloning
nix run github:semi710/naste#deploy -- user@server.example.com

The deploy script:

  1. Pulls the latest Docker image from GHCR
  2. Reads config from /etc/naste-server/env (persists across deploys)
  3. Prompts for PRIVATE_USER, PRIVATE_PASS, PORT on first run
  4. Stops and removes existing container
  5. Starts new container with security hardening (read-only, cap-drop, no-new-privileges)
  6. Config is saved to /etc/naste-server/env with mode 0600

docs.title: Using the Overlay
docs.text:
Add the overlay to your own flake to get pkgs.naste-server and pkgs.naste:

{
  inputs.naste.url = "github:semi710/naste";

  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, naste, ... }: {
    nixpkgs.overlays = [ naste.overlays.default ];

    # Now pkgs.naste-server and pkgs.naste are available
  };
}

docs.title: Justfile Integration
docs.text:
The repo includes a justfile with convenient commands:

just build        # Build server binary
just build-cli    # Build CLI binary
just run          # Run server locally
just run-auth     # Run server with auth
just test         # Run tests
just lint         # golangci-lint + go vet
just image        # Build Docker image via Nix
just image-load   # Build and load into Docker
just deploy       # Deploy locally via Nix
just deploy-remote user@host  # Deploy to remote server
just doc          # Serve docs locally
just clean        # Remove build artifacts

docs.title: NixOS
docs.text: The NixOS module runs naste-server as a hardened systemd service and optionally installs the naste CLI client.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Add the flake input and overlay to your system flake:

{
  inputs.naste.url = "github:semi710/naste";

  outputs = { self, nixpkgs, naste, ... }: {
    nixosConfigurations.myhost = nixpkgs.lib.nixosSystem {
      system = "x86_64-linux";
      modules = [
        naste.nixosModules.default
        {
          nixpkgs.overlays = [ naste.overlays.default ];
          # ... service config here
        }
      ];
    };
  };
}

The overlay provides pkgs.naste-server and pkgs.naste.

docs.title: Quick Setup
docs.text:
{
  services.naste-server = {
    enable = true;
    port = 8080;
    openFirewall = true;
    privateUserFile = config.sops.secrets."naste/user".path;
    privatePassFile = config.sops.secrets."naste/pass".path;
  };

  programs.naste-client = {
    enable = true;
    endpoint = "https://paste.semi.sh";
  };

  sops.secrets."naste/user" = {
    sopsFile = ./secrets/server.yaml;
    group = "naste";
    mode = "0440";
  };
  sops.secrets."naste/pass" = {
    sopsFile = ./secrets/server.yaml;
    group = "naste";
    mode = "0440";
  };
}

docs.title: services.naste-server.enable
docs.text:
Whether to enable the naste paste service. Creates a naste system user/group and a hardened systemd service.

Type: boolean Default: false Example: true

docs.title: services.naste-server.port
docs.text:
HTTP listen port.

Type: port Default: 8080 Example: 9090

docs.title: services.naste-server.dataDir
docs.text:
Storage directory for paste content. Created automatically, owned by the naste user.

Type: path Default: /var/lib/naste-server/data Example: /mnt/storage/naste

Data persistence

Pastes are stored as flat files and never automatically purged. No TTL or expiry. Delete files manually or remove the dataDir.

docs.title: services.naste-server.maxPasteSize
docs.text:
Maximum paste size in bytes. Pastes exceeding this are rejected with HTTP 413.

Type: positive integer Default: 10485760 (10 MB) Example: 52428800 (50 MB)

docs.title: services.naste-server.privateUser
docs.text:
Username for private paste authentication. Empty means public pastes only.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "admin"

docs.title: services.naste-server.privatePass
docs.text:
Password for private paste authentication.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "secret"

docs.title: services.naste-server.privateUserFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the username. Overrides privateUser. Use with sops-nix.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/user"

docs.title: services.naste-server.privatePassFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the password. Overrides privatePass. Use with sops-nix.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/pass"

docs.title: services.naste-server.openFirewall
docs.text:
Whether to open the firewall for the configured port.

Type: boolean Default: false Example: true

docs.title: programs.naste-client.enable
docs.text:
Whether to install the naste CLI client and set the PASTE_ENDPOINT environment variable.

Type: boolean Default: false Example: true

docs.title: programs.naste-client.endpoint
docs.text:
Paste server endpoint URL. Required when enabled. The build fails with an assertion error if not set.

Type: string Default: none (assertion error if unset) Example: "https://paste.semi.sh"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privateUser
docs.text:
Username for private pastes. Sets the PASTE_USER environment variable.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "admin"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privateUserFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the username. Overrides privateUser. Sets the PASTE_USER_FILE environment variable.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/user"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privatePass
docs.text:
Password for private pastes. Sets the PASTE_PASS environment variable.

Type: string Default: "" Example: "secret"

docs.title: programs.naste-client.privatePassFile
docs.text:
Path to a file containing the password. Overrides privatePass. Sets the PASTE_PASS_FILE environment variable.

Type: null or path Default: null Example: "/run/secrets/naste/pass"

docs.title: sops-nix Integration
docs.text:
# secrets/server.yaml (encrypted with sops)
# naste:
#     user: admin
#     pass: your-secret-password

sops.secrets."naste/user" = {
  sopsFile = ./secrets/server.yaml;
  group = "naste";
  mode = "0440";
};
sops.secrets."naste/pass" = {
  sopsFile = ./secrets/server.yaml;
  group = "naste";
  mode = "0440";
};

Secrets are deployed to /run/secrets/naste/user and /run/secrets/naste/pass. The naste group has read access (mode 0440).

docs.title: Service Hardening
docs.text: Setting Value NoNewPrivileges true ProtectSystem strict ProtectHome true PrivateTmp true ReadWritePaths dataDir Restart on-failure RestartSec 5

docs.title: Verifying
docs.text:
systemctl status naste-server.service
curl -s http://localhost:8080/health
journalctl -u naste-server.service -f

docs.title: Reverse Proxy
docs.text: Put naste-server behind a reverse proxy for TLS, custom domains, and rate limiting.

docs.title: Caddy (Recommended)
docs.text:
Caddy provides automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt.

paste.example.com {
    reverse_proxy localhost:8080

    # Rate limiting (requires caddy-ratelimit plugin)
    # rate_limit {
    #     zone naste {
    #         events 10
    #         window 1m
    #     }
    # }
}

Reload Caddy:

systemctl reload caddy

docs.title: Nginx
docs.text:
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name paste.example.com;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name paste.example.com;

    ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/paste.example.com.crt;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/private/paste.example.com.key;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://localhost:8080;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;

        # Max upload size (must match server's 10 MB limit)
        client_max_body_size 10m;
    }
}

docs.title: Tailscale Funnel
docs.text:
Expose naste-server without a domain or TLS certificate using Tailscale Funnel:

# Enable Tailscale funnel on port 8080
sudo tailscale funnel 8080

# Your server is now accessible at:
# https://your-machine.tailnet-name.ts.net

No reverse proxy config needed. Tailscale handles TLS automatically.

docs.title: Tailscale Serve (internal only)
docs.text:
If you only want Tailscale-network access (no public internet):

# Serve on Tailscale network only
tailscale serve 8080

# Accessible at:
# http://your-machine.tailnet-name.ts.net

docs.title: NixOS Caddy Module
docs.text:
If using NixOS with Caddy:

services.caddy = {
  enable = true;
  virtualHosts."paste.example.com".extraConfig = ''
    reverse_proxy localhost:8080
  '';
};

Caddy in NixOS runs as root by default and manages its own TLS certificates.

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• The server sets X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, and CSP headers on all responses
  • For paste views with syntax highlighting, the server overrides CSP to style-src 'unsafe-inline' (required for inline CSS colors)
  • The server reads r.Host directly, not X-Forwarded-Host, to prevent header injection
  • Set client_max_body_size to at least 10m in Nginx to match the server's 10 MB limit
